hyperedges.label: Date-Seeded Rooms Pattern (board, clock, map, daily use daySeed+mulberry32 for SSR/client consistency)
Game Room Juice Stack (sfx + haptics + confetti + achievement toasts on win/lose)
Medallion Pipeline Flow (raw JSONL → bronze → silver → gold → DQ gates)
Game Room → Profile Record → Achievement Toast → Sound/Haptic Feedback Loop
Map Rendering Pipeline: geo lib → WorldMap/GoogleMap → MapGame/DailyGame
Practice Mode: PracticeBar + StreakGame/WedgesGame/MapGame + usePractice hook
Offline-First Triad: seed bank + null Supabase client + seed fallback in queries
ETL Ingest Pipeline: five ingests all call make_fact → dump_raw → upsert_facts
Difficulty computed twice — once in Python forge and once in dbt SQL, both using popularity percentile
Full ETL Pipeline: Extract → Transform (dbt) → Publish
Data Sources → Fact Forge → Typed Questions → Room Renderers
Offline-First Architecture: Bronze JSONL + Seed Bank + World Atlas

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md — Project Instructions for Claude Code
nodes.norm_label: claude.md — project instructions for claude code

nodes.label: Fact→Question Forge Pattern
nodes.rationale: Scraping facts (structured: subject, year, numeric_value, image_url) instead of questions lets one fact fuel multiple game modes. The forge derives typed questions (multiple_choice, year_guess, higher_lower, clue) from the same atomic fact, with difficulty derived from the popularity signal percentile.

nodes.label: Repo-as-Database (DB-less Mode)
nodes.rationale: Bronze JSONL committed to the repo + committed seed-questions.json = production serving path with zero backend accounts. Auto-upgrades to Supabase upserts when secrets are present, enabling multiplayer/leaderboards. Design choice driven by Supabase free-project quota constraints and fitness for this scale.

nodes.label: Content-Hash Idempotent Upserts
nodes.rationale: Facts are keyed on content_hash (sha256 slice of source+subject+text). Idempotent upserts mean re-runs produce minimal diffs in the bronze JSONL and can be safely committed. Deduplication in stg_facts uses a window function on content_hash, latest-wins.

nodes.label: dbt Schema Tests as Publish Gate
nodes.rationale: dbt test failures in the transform job block the publish job via GitHub Actions needs: dependency. Tests are not decoration — they enforce not_null, unique, and accepted_values contracts on the silver and gold layers before any data reaches the seed bank or Supabase.
nodes.norm_label: dbt schema tests as publish gate

nodes.label: One Question Bank, Many Room Renderers
nodes.rationale: Each room is a thin renderer over the same typed question bank. New game = new renderer, zero new data work. Parallels the fantasy tool's seven tools over player_value. Architecture story told in one breath: four different UIs (grid, slider, quiz cards, binary cards) over one data model.
nodes.norm_label: one question bank, many room renderers

nodes.label: Sibling Sampling for Distractor Generation
nodes.rationale: Multiple-choice distractors come from sibling facts: same category, same answer field, within ±1 difficulty band. Type-consistent and plausibility-banded. Wikidata SPARQL can generate these from structured triples (Phase 2 upgrade path).

nodes.label: DAG-Shaped CI Workflow (extract→transform→publish)
nodes.rationale: Workflow jobs follow extract→transform→publish with needs: edges, deliberately mirroring Airflow/Dagster DAG topology. Continue-on-error on each ingest means one flaky API cannot kill the nightly refresh; decay shows up in the category health report instead.

nodes.label: Difficulty Score from Popularity Percentile
nodes.rationale: Difficulty (1-5 scale) is derived from the percentile of the source popularity signal (Wikipedia pageviews, Deezer nb_fan, TMDB popularity) within its category. This makes Jeopardy row values ($200-$1000) fall out automatically and keeps difficulty data-driven rather than hand-labeled.

nodes.label: Anon-Key Read-Only Frontend Convention
nodes.rationale: Frontend never writes to Supabase directly. Scores live in localStorage. The only sanctioned write-path is the submit-score Edge Function which uses the service role internally. RLS enforces public-read on every table. Anon key = frontend reads only. Service-role key = pipeline only.

nodes.label: dbt+DuckDB for Transferable Resume Skills
nodes.rationale: dbt Core + DuckDB chosen as the highest-ROI skill investment: free, runs in-process in CI, skills transfer to Snowflake/Databricks/BigQuery interviews since dbt is the de-facto analytics engineering standard. Deliberate portfolio move: real medallion in a public repo, not a toy.

nodes.label: Databricks Free Edition Mirror as Phase-2 Lab
nodes.rationale: Learning artifact, not serving path. Mirrors dbt/DuckDB medallion onto Databricks Free Edition with Delta Lake + PySpark to demonstrate equivalence (dbt/DuckDB ↔ PySpark/Delta) in interviews. Resume line: Mirrored production ELT pipeline into Databricks Delta tables with medallion architecture. Supabase remains production.
nodes.norm_label: databricks free edition mirror as phase-2 lab

nodes.label: Date-Seeded PRNG for SSR/Client Consistency
nodes.rationale: Daily/shared game setups use lib/rng.ts date-seeded PRNG to ensure SSR and client render the same board, avoiding hydration mismatches. Math.random() is forbidden in SSR paths. Free shuffles only inside click handlers or effects where hydration is not a concern.

nodes.label: Supabase Realtime Broadcast for Multiplayer Lobby
nodes.rationale: Phase 4 multiplayer uses Supabase Realtime Broadcast (ephemeral, no DB rows written). Host drives state machine; players mirror host broadcasts. Presence tracks connected player names. Degrades gracefully to a requires-backend notice when NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL is absent, keeping all other rooms playable.
nodes.norm_label: supabase realtime broadcast for multiplayer lobby

nodes.label: Category Neon Color System
nodes.rationale: Six category colors (history amber, music magenta, sports green, screen blue, geography teal, wildcard violet) serve as both the Trivial Pursuit wedge palette and the room neon signage. Single source in lib/types.ts CATEGORY_HEX + tailwind safelist. Color never used as the only signal per accessibility rules.
